relative_path: Management & planning/Background, framework and strategic goals/Cell_cycle_ project framework.md
full_text:
**Goal**: Spatial profiling of therapeutically targetable, cell
cycle-related molecules in Post-NACT HGSC HRP patients

Strengths:

- Cell cycle-related spatial biology has never been studied within
 > HGSC

- Many vulnerabilities (CCNE amp, DDR) are key targets that could
 > potentially succeed in the treatment

- Most of the usable markers are nuclear stainings, which eases the
 > process

- Cell fate is found within the intersection of many other processes
 > that can be also studied in the same cohort (metabolism, DNA
 > damage etc)

Potential risks of treatments of interest:

- 1\) off-target inhibitory effect on close family members of target
 > protein

- 2\) acquired drug resistance due to mutants on target or upstream
 > regulators

- 3\) many of these drugs are still under development

Technical considerations (DNA vs S phase marker, dimensionality
reduction with the cyclins?):

- System to classify arrested/cycling/proliferating cells

- Classification of cell cycle stages

- Profile immune activation/inactivation/escape/exclusion

- Non-canonical patterns need to be adressed - Ilastik?

- We are working on 2D slides (cannot quantify full cell
 > content/morphology)

![](media/image1.png){width="5.817708880139983in"
height="2.8268471128608925in"}

**Elements of interest:**

> Basic TME phenotyping + immune checkpoints
>
> Cyclins/cdk's:

ogy)

![](media/image1.png){width="5.817708880139983in"
height="2.8268471128608925in"}

**Elements of interest:**

> Basic TME phenotyping + immune checkpoints
>
> Cyclins/cdk's:

- **CDK4/6 Inhibitors** - I believe are promising in combination with
 > IO treatments

 - **Palbociclib, ribociclib,
 > abemaciclib**![](media/image2.png){width="2.026042213473316in"
 > height="1.7292333770778652in"}

 - Rb intact tumors are more sensitive

 - mTOR pathway can cause resistance

 - presence/accumulation of cyclin E and CDK2 are adaptations that
 > cause resistance (BC)

 - In PALOMA trials they concluded that it:

 - Causes cell cycle arrest

 - Decreases CD4+ FOXP3+ T reg/CD8+ T cell ratio

 - Increases IFN III levels and T cell activation

 - Increases PD-L1 and impairs DDR through HR

- **CCND1-related treatments**

 - GSK3b is crucial for its degradation

 - **Metformin** (AMPK activator, glycolisis inhibitor)**-** causes
 > cell cycle arrest by decreasing CCND1 levels through AMPK
 > GSKbeta levels (suppressing AKT)

 - **Mk-2206, Perfosine** Pan AKT inhibitors might work but they
 > are not clinically approved

- **CCNE**

 - There are common amplifications in these patients

 - Overexpression deregulates the cell cycle at G1/S and G2/M by
 > accelerating S phase entry, causes replication stress

 - Confers resistance to platinum therapy in HGSC

ents

 - Overexpression deregulates the cell cycle at G1/S and G2/M by
 > accelerating S phase entry, causes replication stress

 - Confers resistance to platinum therapy in HGSC

 - CCNEamp tumors rely on HR to repair collapsed replication forks

 - Inhibits anoikis through FOXO family proteins

 - 'degrader' compounds

- **Cdk2 inhibitors**

 - Still under debate

 - ATP binding socket is shared with CDK1, and this might
 > induce toxicity

 - CDK2 degraders have been developed instead, better
 > antiproliferative effects

 - Regulates G1-S checkpoint

 - Inhibition of CDK2 at low doses sensitizes CCNE1-amplified cells
 > to DNA-damaging agents both in vitro and in vivo

> Replication checkpoint

- ATR pathways

 - **Chk1 inhibitors**

 - **ATR inhibitors**

> Checkpoint recovery

- **Inhibition of WEE1** (phosphorylates cdk1 to prevent mitotic
 > entry)

 - **Adavosertib**: promising results in uterine serous carcinoma

 - Maybe good option for CCNE amplified HRP
 > HGSC:![](media/image4.png){width="2.647603893263342in"
 > height="1.8115179352580928in"}

 - CCNE overexpression causes DNA origin firing and induces
 > re-replication (DNA replication stress)

 - We can examine Rad51, 53BP1, gH2Ax

 - [Dinaciclib]{.underline} (a CDK1/2/5/9 inhibitor) together with
 > AZD1775 (WEE1 inhibitor), was causing more killing than
 > WEE1inh alone in CNNE-low MDA231 cells

- Aurora-A

- [Dinaciclib]{.underline} (a CDK1/2/5/9 inhibitor) together with
 > AZD1775 (WEE1 inhibitor), was causing more killing than
 > WEE1inh alone in CNNE-low MDA231 cells

- Aurora-A

- PLK1(pro-mitotic) inhibitors

- In lung cancer WEE1 inhibitors upregulated PD-L1

> ![](media/image3.png){width="5.078125546806649in"
> height="3.1379779090113735in"}
>
> Apart from functional markers, I need two strategies:

- 

**Sample selection**

- HRP Patients

- Good % of tumor

- Some of them with CyclinE amp

- I have no idea about whether we have Rb status but it would be
 > important

- Post-NACT

- Tonsil+Spleen

- Cases with low fat content

 - Gray ones (meh)

- Between 30-50 patients

 - The interval samples in that list are good!

 - The ones that are not in the list have few tumor

 - HRD status

 - 10 patients for Ashwini's manuscript (WES-PDS)
